title: Electroporation & Electrocompetent Cell Preparation Protocol for DAP auxotroph E.coli WM3064
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d5ecd","text":"This is a protocol for making electrocompetent E.coli WM3064 cells and transforming them with DNA through electroporation. This protocol produces    aliquots of electrocompetent cells, each enough for one prep of electroporation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":146,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"30","error":false,"guid":"42487091D42911EFAF540A58A9FEAC02","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":1}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7qe2c","text":"Electroporation Protocol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"72eq2","text":"The electroporation protocol will vary depending on the strain so this protocol may need to be optimized.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dr92g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e39cb","text":"Transformation efficiency calculation","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":37}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8c2lp","text":"If the culture was diluted 1000-fold when plated, the total cfu per ml is 1000 times the number of colonies counted. The cfu is divided by the amount of plasmid (e.g. 10 pg per ml)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"apfpc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"708u9","text":"Transformation efficiency = cfu/ µg = (colonies counted*1000) / (0.00001 µg plasmid)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1j3gg","text":"Quite a bit of glycerol gets left behind in the tube during the washing steps while discarding the supernatant. Note that this dilutes the cells a bit. Prepare aliquots based on the cell quantity required specifically for your use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cer1i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3jarb","text":"Dilute the plasmid to have 1 - 3 µL of DNA (\u003c100 ng) per electroporation prep. Calculate the amount of DNA used per prep to determine the transformation efficiency later.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"27d7c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1hmb0","text":"The electroporation mixture ratio will vary depending on the strain and DNA size so this protocol may need to be optimized.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2tusn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7mplm","text":"Make sure to include a negative control which also serves as a \"survival control\". This mixture should contain only electrocompetent cells and no plasmid. Plate this mixture on LB+DAP agar to assess whether the cells survive the electroporation pulse. steps: Turn on electroporator and set to 1.5 kv , 200 ohms and 25 µF .
Pre-warm recovery SOC+DAP medium and LB-antibiotic plates at 37 °C .
Thaw microcentrifuge tubes with electrocompetent cells On ice or use freshly made cells.
Place appropriate number of 1 mm-electroporation cuvettes On ice .

Add 1 µL, DNA solution ( to 30 µL electrocompetent cells in microcentrifuge tubes. This mixture can be optimized for your strain and plasmid. Trying a few combination ratios is recommended in order to determine which one yields the highest transformation efficiency. A lower amount of DNA usually yields higher efficiency.
Transfer the DNA-cell mixture to the cold cuvette, tap on countertop 2X, wipe water from exterior of cuvette and place in the electroporation module and press pulse ( don’t hold the button down ).

Immediately add 970 µL, 37°C SOC + DAP , mix by pipetting up and down once and transfer to a microcentrifuge tube. Try to add the recovery medium as soon as possible. Transformation efficiency sharply declines with increase in time between pulse and addition of recovery medium.
Incubate at 37 °C for 01:00:00 .
Make appropriate dilutions and plate 100 µL on LB agar + DAP + antibiotic plates.

Incubate Overnight at 37 °C . Transformation efficiency calculation: If the culture was diluted 1000-fold when plated, the total cfu per ml is 1000 times the number of colonies counted. The cfu is divided by the amount of plasmid (e.g. 10 pg per ml) Transformation efficiency = cfu/ µg = (colonies counted*1000) / (0.00001 µg plasmid)
Add a loopful of bacterial culture to 5 mL LB broth.
Incubate Overnight at 37 °C with shaking.
Add 10 mL SOB medium containing 0.3 millimolar (mM) 2,6-Diaminopimelic acid Thermo Scientific in a 50 mL conical flask.
Inoculate each flask with overnight culture to OD600 = 0.05
Grow cells until mid-exponential phase (OD600 = 0.6) or 02:00:00
Transfer the cells to a 15 mL Falcon tube
Chill the culture On ice for 00:10:00 .
Centrifuge the cells at 6000 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Discard the supernatant.
Wash the pellet by adding 10 mL chilled 10 % volume Glycerol Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #17904 and vortex vigourously.
Centrifuge 6000 rpm, 4 °C, 3 minutes 30 seconds and discard the supernatant.
go to step #10 .
Resuspend the washed pellet in 100 µL 10 % volume Glycerol Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #17904
Make 30 µL aliquots in sterile microcentrifuge tubes. Proceed to electroporation or store at -80 °C . One tube allows for one prep of electroporation.
creator: Atharva Karde
